title: Sacramento
prompt_preview:
[1 chunks] Task: Extract all distinct claims about AI or emerging technology in this press release. Do NOT assume a fixed number.
Output JSON only matching the provided schema.
Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem

claims.claim_text: In California, drowning is the leading cause of death for children ages 1-4 and second for children ages 5-14.
For every child who dies from drowning, 7 more receive emergency care for non-fatal drownings.
Participation in formal swimming lessons lowers the risk of drowning for children ages 1-4 by 88%.
Drowning causes significant financial costs associated with emergency responders, hospitalization, follow-up healthcare, and special education.
79% of children in households with incomes less than $50,000 have few or no swimming skills.
Parents who don’t know how to swim are much more likely to have children who also lack swimming skills.
African-American children have a drowning death rate among ages 5-9 that is more than 2.5 times higher than the rest of the population.
The drowning death rate grows to 3.5 times higher among African-American children ages 10-14.
Drowning is the leading cause of death for children and adults with autism, who are nearly 40 times more likely to drown than the general population.
Educating parents and providing no-cost swim lessons for children will create a culture of water safety and prevent drownings.
California has 840 miles of coastline, 3,000 lakes, 1,400 reservoirs, 50,000 ponds, and more than 1.3 million swimming pools.
